body:
Hi @DB-Alex,

Thank you for raising this issue.

Sorry but we need an actual reproduction for this issue as it's pretty specific, it's hard to tell without knowing what your `getStaticProps` implementation does, or how `InstantSearch` is being used. 
Normally it shouldn't have to call `dispose` unless you're going on a route without `InstantSearch` at all. Are you changing the `key` based on the route ?

The line you mentioned is here for existing apps, and there might be a workaround we can do in your app, if we have a reproduction.
Hi @aymeric-giraudet the product pages in my case dont have InstantSearch at all. But they are on the same catch all next route.

Hi @DB-Alex,

Do you mean you have a condition in which based on the slug caught in `[...all].js`, you conditionally render either a Hit page or an `InstantSearch` page ? Then it would make sense than dispose is called indeed.

However would it be possible to still declare an actual route for InstantSearch ? Like having a predefined `search.js` page for it then the rest can be caught in `[...all].js` for hits, maybe that would fix things ?

Once again it's a bit difficult to tell without a reproduction, providing one would help us greatly for fixing this :)

Hi @DB-Alex,

I think I might have reproduced your issue. As you're using `getStaticProps` I assume you had to make a custom `createURL` and `parseURL` as it's impossible to get query params in `getStaticProps`.

When `this.write` is called in dispose it actually checks multiple things, one of them is that it checks if `createURL` yields the same result as `window.location.href`, in which case it will not write.

Here's a quick and dirty reproduction I did, you have to go to `/search` to see the page : https://codesandbox.io/s/cocky-darkness-qhf7f0?file=/pages/%5B...all%5D.js:2081-2084
If the route starts with `/product-` it will display a product page, else if you're on something like `/search` it will display a search page. 
Could you please modify it to be close to your implementation, especially `createURL` and `parseURL` (those I made are very simple and naive implementations)

Overall I think if it were possible for you to have separate pages it would be much easier and you wouldn't even need to have custom `createURL` and `parseURL`, but I'm aware it's not always easy in real life apps 😅

Thanks @DB-Alex !

I think this is because this `createURL` implem does not take `location.protocol` and `location.host` into account.

To debug you could add this to `node_modules/instantsearch.js/es/lib/routers/history.js` at line 257 (inside `shouldWrite` basically) : 
```js
console.log(url, window.location.href);
```

And change your `createURL` implem so that the two strings are the same when you go on a hit page. I suspect for now it will be something like `/product-xxx` and `http://localhost/product-xxx` which is why it writes when it shouldn't.

Hope that helps !

Hi @aymeric-giraudet,

That was indeed the case! Thanks for your help :D

I think I was to early. Now the URLS are exactly the same but I get this warning:

```
The UI state for the index "v3_products" is not consistent with the widgets mounted.
is can happen when the UI state is specified via `initialUiState`, `routing` or `setUiState` but that the widgets responsible for this state were not added. This results in those query parameters not being sent to the API.
```

Of course all the virtual widgets are there...
Nevermind component was not unmounting, everything works as expected!
